name: state-management
capabilities: TanStack Query patterns for server state including query keys factory and mutations
Zustand patterns for client state with typed stores and persist middleware
React Hook Form + Zod patterns for type-safe form validation
Performance optimization using selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders
Optimistic updates and cache invalidation strategies
URL state management with nuqs or searchParams patterns
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only TypeScript code patterns for state management. All 53 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner incorrectly identified TypeScript template literals (backticks) as Ruby shell execution, repository URLs as network vulnerabilities, and standard metadata fields as C2 keywords. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or external commands exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Managing server state and client state in React applications creates complexity that slows development and hurts user experience. This skill provides proven patterns for separating server state from client state using TanStack Query for data fetching and Zustand for UI state.
limitations: Contains documentation and code patterns only, no runtime code execution
Does not generate complete project boilerplate or scaffolding
Assumes existing React or Next.js project with dependencies installed

useCases.title: Fetch and cache server data
useCases.description: Implement data fetching with TanStack Query including cache management and background refetching

useCases.title: Manage UI state globally
useCases.description: Create client-side state stores with Zustand for theme, sidebar, and user preferences

useCases.title: Build validated forms
useCases.description: Create type-safe forms with React Hook Form and Zod validation

promptTemplates.title: Basic Query Hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a TanStack Query hook to fetch users from /api/users endpoint with loading and error states

promptTemplates.title: Persisted Store
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a Zustand store with persist middleware to save user theme preference to localStorage

promptTemplates.title: Query Keys Factory
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a query keys factory for a posts feature with support for list, detail, and filter queries

promptTemplates.title: Optimized Selectors
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to use Zustand selectors properly to prevent unnecessary re-renders in a large application

bestPractices: Separate server state from client state concerns using appropriate libraries
Use selectors in Zustand to select only the state slices components need
Implement optimistic updates for mutations to improve perceived performance
antiPatterns: Storing server-fetched data in global client state like Zustand
Using the entire store instead of selectors causing unnecessary re-renders
Fetching data directly in components without caching or TanStack Query

faq.question: What React versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with React 18+ and Next.js App Router. Requires TypeScript 5.0+ for best type inference.

faq.question: What are the performance limits?
faq.answer: Pattern guidance follows library recommendations. Large apps benefit from selector-based re-render optimization.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Next.js?
faq.answer: Compatible with Server Components and Client Components. TanStack Query prefetching works in loaders.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Documentation only skill. No code execution, no network calls, no file system access beyond reading patterns.

faq.question: Why is my component re-rendering too much?
faq.answer: Use Zustand selectors to select specific state slices. Avoid destructuring the entire store in components.

faq.question: When should I use TanStack Query vs Zustand?
faq.answer: Use TanStack Query for server state (API data). Use Zustand for client state (UI, preferences, session).
